- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/iw2NkT3SK2iDl8C30NNH_1531636_1385137178457210_2804924682837888721_o.jpeg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/iw2NkT3SK2iDl8C30NNH_1531636_1385137178457210_2804924682837888721_o.jpeg)

---

##    4\. Finding Your Message | Modern Day Geisha (Part 1) 

Do you know what Vision Quest is?

Not the video game.

Not the movie.

No, the Native American ritual that young people have to go through in order to become adults.

Basically it goes like this... 

The kid is sent out into nature alone... naked... with no food... and they're not allowed to come back to the village for 4 days and 4 nights. During that time they're supposed to pray and cry out to the Gods... Until they finally see their "vision".

That "vision" may be in the form of a dream. Or it can be from symbols that they see in animal crossings or in the elements of nature.

The "vision" they get tells them what their purpose in life is. It outlines their role in their community. It gives them absolute clarity on how they may best serve their people.

And only after they get this vision can come back.

My friend Beki decided to go on a 3 night Vision Quest with me last month.

She's about to turn 25 and decided to figure out what her message was.

I've done this before with entrepreneurs, singers, rappers and brands.

But never with a model.

Before I did it people would ask me "Are models even supposed to have a message?"

Find out in Part 1 of this new controversial video we shot. 

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

thank you for sharing this! This was very inspiring. I just realised that I walked in Berlin at Maybachufer a few years ago and we crossed paths. We didnt interact but I knew I have seen her somewhere online. I just remembered that now that I saw her in this video. We share the same favourite books and I felt like our paths and emotional landscapes are so familiar. Synchronicities, I guess.

![Krissy Whiski](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/2f807be443d0c817703b320742b3769e?d=mm&s=70)

Krissy Whiski

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8886468/comments/23567148)

As a former dancer myself who has been on a 6 year journey as an artist I felt like I was listening to a doppelgänger of my younger self. One of my favorite books is also the Alchemist and I teared up at Beki’s truth because damn do I know that reality that was where I was 10 years ago. I’m in a different place in my journey now, but I know that pain and how much work it takes to prove to yourself and everyone around you that you have more layers, more value and more to offer than just being an object of desire.

Little bit, day by day it’s unfolding. I’ve been able to integrate so many of your messages. Where I struggle right now is making the time around being a mom first to 3 kids 5 and under. My time isn’t all mine right now and the simple answer of getting up earlier or staying up later doesn’t fit my puzzle right now.

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8886468/comments/22898806)

Forever trying to find what my message is and until watching this, I didn’t know that it’s simply to be a fucking badass- whatever that means to each individual . To be real with yourself And others, to be okay with standing out for being different. For me it’s to express myself via spraypaint and skating and to inspire other girls and women to trash the stigmas of “lady like”.
